title: Deepen Elder Scrolls simulation with lore-aware locations, NPCs, items, and prompts
body:
Expanded Elder Scrolls depth by infusing locations, encounters, NPCs, and generation prompts with province/guild/Daedric context; added iconic destinations and items so generated narrative and imagery stay firmly Tamrielic.

- **Lore systems & prompts**: Added `LORE_PRIMER` and `ELDER_SCROLLS_LORE_SYSTEMS`, injected into text workers/prompts; location lore hooks now surface in prompt context; image prompts include province/faction/landmark tags for grounded visuals.
- **World data**: Enriched all existing locations with lore metadata; introduced new destinations (Whiterun, College of Winterhold, Blackreach, Vivec Foreign Quarter, Imperial City) and travel actions; new encounters (dragon overflight, Thalmor patrol, Daedric cult) tied to lore.
- **NPCs & items**: Added key NPCs (Maven Black-Briar, Aela, Farengar, Savos Aren, Faralda, M’aiq, Ordinator, Imperial Centurion) and slotted them into relevant locales; expanded item set with Tamrielic staples (sweetroll, skooma, soul gems, welkynd stone) and artifact-aware legendary stat generation.
- **Image/text coherence**: Image prompt builder now factors province/factions/landmarks; text workers in all variants mirror lore matrices and location hooks for consistent generation output.

Example (prompt injection):
```ts
const lorePrimer = LORE_PRIMER;
const loreSystems = ELDER_SCROLLS_LORE_SYSTEMS;
const locationLore = state.world.current_location.lore || {};
const locationLoreStr = Object.keys(locationLore).length ? JSON.stringify(locationLore) : "No special lore hooks";
// Lore Primer / Systems / Location Lore Hooks now included in the prompt body
```

title: Hyper-realistic SVG sprite enhancements: SSS, iris detail, race textures, anatomical hands/feet
body:
Brings the SVG character model closer to DoL-level sprite fidelity with physically-based rendering techniques and anatomical detail.

### Rendering pipeline additions
- **Subsurface scattering filter** (`#sss-filter`) — race-adaptive warm light transmission (green for Argonian, warm for Khajiit, red/orange for humans)
- **Skin texture noise** via `feTurbulence` fractal noise overlay
- **Dual-edge rim lighting** — main light (left) + fill light (right) with per-limb rim paths

### Eye overhaul
- Sclera radial gradient with blood vessel hints, limbal ring (dark iris border), iris fiber detail (radial lines), corneal reflection arc
- Race-specific pupils: vertical slits for Khajiit/Argonian via `<ellipse>`, round for all others
- Lid crease shadow, lower waterline highlight, 6 varied-length lashes per eye

### Race-specific body textures
- **Argonian**: `<pattern>` diamond scale overlay on torso/limbs, belly plate lines
- **Khajiit**: Directional fur stripe pattern, chest tuft/ruff detail
- Skin vein hints (wrist, inner elbow, temple) on non-beast races

### Anatomical detail
- **Hands**: Palm crease lines, per-finger nail beds with `#nail-grad`, knuckle creases, fingertip pads
- **Feet**: Achilles tendon, lateral malleolus, individual toe separation with toenails, arch shadow
- **Arms**: Deltoid separation, bicep/tricep contours, forearm tendon lines, ulna/radius wrist bones
- **Lips**: `#lip-grad` radial gradient, cupid's bow highlight, moisture shine ellipse

### Animations (CSS classes)
`anim-tail-wag`, `anim-hair-sway`, `anim-whisker-twitch`, `anim-sweat-drip`, `anim-corruption-pulse` — all extracted to `index.css` utility classes, referenced by `className` instead of inline styles.

### Conditional overlays
- Goosebump scatter at high stress/arousal (`> 0.8 maxStress` or `> 0.6 maxArousal`)
- Enhanced breathing animation with `translateY` for chest rise/fall

title: Character model: realistic body forms (mys_dol parity)
body:
SVG sidebar model body silhouettes were overly simplified — column-like torso, near-zero thigh width, straight rectangular neck, spherical breasts. Redraws key body paths to match mys_dol proportional style.

## Torso
- **Female**: 10-segment cubic bezier hourglass — shoulder (x=68/132) → waist narrows (x=74/126) → hips flare *wider than shoulders* (x=66/134) → crotch (x=86/114). Shape is in the fill path, not just shadow overlays.
- **Male**: V-taper — shoulders x=64/136, waist x=78/122, minimal hip flare, ~1.8:1 shoulder:hip ratio.

## Legs
- **Female**: Thigh width ~22px (was ~10px). Outer edge S-curves: thigh x=70 → knee x=78 → calf bulge → ankle x=84. Added outer hip/glute contour ellipses, inner-thigh gap shadows, calf highlights at corrected positions.
- **Male**: Outer thigh to x=72, prominent calf, quad definition lines.

## Neck
Replaced `<rect>` with gender-variant Q-bezier `<path>`: female 26px chin → 22px collar; male 28px → 24px.

## Breasts
`<circle r=18>` → `<ellipse rx=19 ry=22>` — slightly taller than wide, gravity-lowered center (cy 160→164). Areola positions and underboob shadow updated accordingly.

## Arms
Gender-variant fill silhouettes: female slender (outer to x≈30), male wider (x≈26). Proper bicep-bulge outer curve replaces old flat `L 45 220` wrist joint.

title: Agentic Full-Stack Development & Automation of MaxVP via GitHub
body:
## Goal
Agentically and autonomously use all features of GitHub to fully develop the entire MaxVP product in the romeytheAI/Ga-mg repo, leveraging automation and full-stack capabilities. This includes setting up and managing projects, utilizing code spaces, spinning up/building agents, opening/managing issues, pull requests, handling pushes, writing comments, facilitating reviews, and orchestrating all activities for continuous, automated advancement.

## Tasks
- Set up GitHub Projects for MaxVP and automate project management
- Configure and use GitHub Codespaces for consistent dev environments
- Build or connect agents that autonomously:
  - Create and assign issues
  - Open/code pull requests
  - Handle pushes to repo
  - Perform code reviews/commentary/discussion
- Track progress and maintain documentation via issues and project boards
- Use GitHub Actions (or agent workflows) to automate CI/CD and other repository operations
- Enable or configure additional integrations to ensure fully automated agentic teamwork and feedback loops
- Ensure all parts of MaxVP are developed, tracked, and improved via these mechanisms

## Automation & Orchestration Example (Code)
Below is a code snippet for launching an agentic workflow—this can serve as a starting point for expanding automation in the repo:

```typescript
// Example: Submitting a job to an agentic service (from src/sim/SimulationEngine.ts)
async function submitHordeJob(prompt: string, apiKey: string): Promise<string> {
  const resp = await fetch(`${HORDE_API}/generate/text/async`, {
    method: 'POST',
    headers: {
      'Content-Type': 'application/json',
      'apikey': apiKey || '0000000000',
    },
    body: JSON.stringify({
      prompt,
      params: { max_length: 200, max_context_length: 1024 },
      models: ['koboldcpp/Llama-3-8B-Lexi-Uncensored_Q4_K_M'],
    }),
  });
  if (!resp.ok) throw new Error(`Horde submit failed: ${resp.status}`);
  const data = await resp.json();
  return data.id as string;
}
```
- See `src/sim/SimulationEngine.ts` for more details on agentic job management.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Projects, Codespaces, automation agents, and full-stack workflows are fully operating autonomously
- Agents can handle all code/PR/issue/review lifecycle steps without constant human direction
- Documentation and operation history are transparent via GitHub
- MaxVP can be iterated and improved continuously using these agentic processes

---
Use or expand workflow, agent, or orchestration scripts (see `src/sim/SimulationEngine.ts`, `vite.config.ts`, etc.) to achieve the above.

title: Epic: Achieve DOL-inspired full systems and graphics/content parity
body:
Goal: Replicate feature, content, and graphics parity with DOL (core gameplay, UI, all content and event coverage).
This epic tracks all issues required to:
- Build core simulation, state model, time systems, and event/scene engine
- Match rendering and UI (graphics parity)
- Implement full content coverage (scenes, encounters, events, progression)
- Achieve parity in save/load, inventory, stats, conditions, world systems, and authoring tools
- Organize all implementation issues by milestone (MVP, Core Loop, World, Event Engine, Graphics, Content, QA, Launch Readiness)

All implementation issues are sub-issues attached to this epic.

Acceptance:
- All implementation issues closed
- Milestones closed sequentially
- Demo confirms full parity vs. DOL
- All code reviewed, test passing, basic QA complete

title: Implement player stats, needs, and conditions system
body:
Define and implement player stats/needs (energy, hunger, arousal, etc.), conditions (wounds, buffs/debuffs), including UI display and effect hooks.

Acceptance:
- Stats/conditions tracked in game state
- Core triggers/effects settable by events/choices
- Stats update with sim tick
- Loss/failure/recovery handling ready for content

Epic: #epic-dol-parity

title: Implement in-game time and calendar system
body:
Design and implement a time tracking system: current day, time of day, passage, calendar events, and hooks for time-based effects.

Acceptance:
- Time-of-day and day progression wired to sim loop
- Time-based events can trigger and expire
- UI updates for current time
- Edge cases: midnight events, forced waits, etc.

Epic: #epic-dol-parity

title: Implement world/map structure and navigation system
body:
Build the world map representation (location graph, zones, connections). Implement navigation logic for player movement, location unlocks, triggers for on-enter/on-leave, and UI interaction.

Acceptance:
- Map structure supports all required DOL navigation/rooms
- API supports queries for adjacent/available nodes
- Navigation actions update/discover state
- UI map and movement implemented, updated with player state
- Event/encounter triggers on entry/exit points

Epic: #epic-dol-parity

title: Design and build inventory and equipment system
body:
Implement inventory management (add/remove items, equip/unequip, stackable/single, capacity, UI for list/equip). Model supports item events/effects as per DOL, with clear serialization.

Acceptance:
- Full add/remove, stack, equip/unequip logic
- Durable/integrated with core game state & save
- UI for browsing inventory and equipping
- Effect hooks wired for later content

Epic: #epic-dol-parity

title: Migrate all DOL content to structured YAML/JSON scenes/events
body:
Bulk import, migrate, or build out the full set of DOL scenes, encounters, story branches, using new data-driven event/scene engine. Ensure all content coverage, test migration, verify hooks for all features.

Acceptance:
- All DOL content defined as structured scene/event YAML/JSON
- Content validated, parsed, and loaded without runtime errors
- Scene requirements/outcomes validated
- Coverage matches source DOL (core, events, progression, endings)
- Migration tooling or documentation provided

Epic: #epic-dol-parity

title: Build rendering system for graphical parity with DOL
body:
Implement rendering architecture (canvas, sprites, overlays). Support DOL-style backgrounds, character sprites, UI overlays, and graphical transitions/animation between state changes, encounters, and feedback.

Acceptance:
- Graphic scenes and overlays match DOL layout & fidelity
- Sprites/scene assets composited by state/event requirements
- Animation and state transitions handled smoothly
- Layout adapts to mobile/desktop
- Test assets or placeholder visuals provided

Epic: #epic-dol-parity

title: Implement event/scene engine for branching narrative
body:
Create the event/scene presentation engine: YAML/JSON schema to define scenes, events, requirements and outcomes. Implement runtime branching, requirements, choices, consequences, state changes, and script hooks.

Acceptance:
- Events/scenes defined as data, including requirements & outcomes
- Engine supports multi-choice, conditional branches, events with chained triggers
- UI displays choices, feedback, and consequences clearly
- Hooks for stats/inv/world/persistence

Epic: #epic-dol-parity

title: Implement crime, bounty, and law enforcement system

Design a legal-state framework for theft, trespassing, assault, contraband, and faction-specific law violations. Support local bounty tracking, witness reporting, and guard escalation.

Acceptance:
- Crimes are categorized and recorded against region or faction authority
- Bounty values escalate based on severity and repeat behavior
- Witness logic can trigger reports and delayed guard response
- Guards and law actors react based on bounty tier and current context
- Quest and event systems can create or clear crimes and bounties

title: Implement weather and environmental condition system
body:
Add weather, temperature, seasonality, visibility, and environmental modifiers that affect travel, encounters, survival stats, schedules, and scene availability.

- Environmental conditions can modify event weighting and travel outcomes
- UI exposes current weather and short-term forecast/context

title: Implement event eligibility and requirement rules engine
body:

- Rules are data-driven and serializable for authored and generated content

- Automated tests cover edge cases and complex combinations

Epic: #epic-dol-parity

title: Build journal, map, and HUD shell for full gameplay loop
body:
Create the core shell UI for navigation, survival stats, quest journal, notifications, faction status, time/weather, and contextual actions.

title: Implement dynamic event weighting, cooldowns, and anti-repetition logic

Release notes

Sourced from requests's releases.

v2.33.0

2.33.0 (2026-03-25)

Announcements

  • 📣 Requests is adding inline types. If you have a typed code base that uses Requests, please take a look at #7271. Give it a try, and report any gaps or feedback you may have in the issue. 📣

Security

  • CVE-2026-25645 requests.utils.extract_zipped_paths now extracts contents to a non-deterministic location to prevent malicious file replacement. This does not affect default usage of Requests, only applications calling the utility function directly.

Improvements

  • Migrated to a PEP 517 build system using setuptools. (#7012)

Bugfixes

  • Fixed an issue where an empty netrc entry could cause malformed authentication to be applied to Requests on Python 3.11+. (#7205)

title: ⚡ Bolt: [performance improvement] Eliminate unnecessary React re-renders and RegExp allocations
body:
💡 **What:** 
1. Added custom `React.memo` comparator functions to `NarrativeLog.tsx`, `StatBar`, and `SectionHeader` in `DoLStatsSidebar.tsx`.
2. Extracted expensive `new RegExp(word, 'gi')` instantiations out of a hot loop in `filterNarrativeByIgnorance` (`src/sim/DiscoverySystem.ts`) into a static module-level array `IGNORANCE_MASKS`.

🎯 **Why:** 
1. `NarrativeLog` was re-rendering constantly because its `state` prop is a massive global object whose reference changes on every tick. The new comparator correctly narrows the check to the only nested property it cares about (`state.player.knowledge.sexual_awareness`).
2. `StatBar` and `SectionHeader` were failing `React.memo` checks because their parent passed inline React elements to their `icon` props (e.g., `icon={<Heart />}`), causing reference changes every render. The new comparators explicitly ignore these inline nodes and check only primitive props.
3. `new RegExp()` was compiling multiple times per word on every re-render of the massive narrative log list. Pre-compiling to a static array eliminates this hidden O(N) allocation cost.

📊 **Impact:** 
- Massive reduction in unnecessary DOM recalculations and React fiber reconciliations for the sidebar UI and narrative log.
- Drops continuous regex allocation cost from the text rendering pipeline.

🔬 **Measurement:** 
Run the app, open the game UI, and observe React DevTools Profiler during state ticks. The sidebar stats and the previous log entries will now correctly skip render cycles.

---
*PR created automatically by Jules for task [16396512581897724352](https://jules.google.com/task/16396512581897724352) started by @romeytheAI*
